428 J Street r Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 e Fax (916) 322-0886
October 29,2003
Nancy C. Miller
Miller, Owen & Trost
428 J Street, Suite 400
Sacramento, CA 9 581 4-239 4
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-03-249
Dear Ms. Miller:
We are in receipt of your request for confirmation of compliance under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Actl by San Benito County
Supervisor Richard Scagliotti. Note that regulation 18329@)(7)(copy enclosed) provides
that immunity is only conferred through formal written advice, not oial advice.2 After a
careful review of the facts you submitted, we decline to provide written advice to you at
this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending
investigation by the Enforcernent Division. Your question involves urutyris oidecisions,
which have already occurred, such that issuing written advice at this time would relate to
past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of
the Act so mandated by section 831 I 1.
Should you have any additional questions on this matter, please contact the
Commission at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
W. Wallace
General Counsel
Jwwjg
Enclosure
I Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections lgl09-
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
' Moreover, this letter should not bJconstrued to accept as accurate your summary of any oral advice
provided. Since we are not rendering written advice, itls not appropriate to address the accuracy ofthe
facts concerning the advice for which you seek confirmation.
i
Re:

sections.conclusion: We decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. Your question involves analysis of decisions, which have already occurred, such that issuing written advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether San Benito County Supervisor Richard Scagliotti's conduct complies with the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the issues are the subject of a pending Enforcement Division investigation, and providing advice on past conduct would not serve the impartial administration of the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections are present with headers, but the request and reasoning for declination are clearly stated in the body. File number W-03-249 suggests a withdrawal/declination (W prefix). Footnotes provide regulatory context.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether San Benito County Supervisor Richard Scagliotti's conduct complies with the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: We decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. Your question involves analysis of decisions, which have already occurred, such that issuing written advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide written advice regarding San Benito County Supervisor Richard Scagliotti's compliance with conflict-of-interest provisions, citing a pending Enforcement Division investigation into the same or similar issues.
